title: Heat dome over Europe scorches UK, France, Spain
subhed: The French capital, Paris, on Saturday hit 31.9°C, its highest temperature of the year. Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu will hold a meeting Thursday with key ministers to go over government preparations for the heatwave.

Senegal names economist Lo as new PM after Sonko ouster
subhed: President Bassirou Diomaye Faye named Ahmadou Al Aminou Lo as the head of government, replacing Ousmane Sonko who was fired on Friday, following months of simmering tensions between the two men.

title: French airlines are finally turning to sustainable fuels to break free from fossil fuel dependence
subhed: Sustainable aviation fuels, which do not require any extraction of fossil carbon, are now being seen as an opportunity by airlines. However, SAFs remain extremely expensive, and the available supply is insufficient.

title: Roland-Garros: How players are adapting to the scorching heat on the courts
subhed: The Paris Grand Slam tournament, which began on May 24, is facing an early heatwave that is sweeping across France. Not all athletes are equally equipped to handle these oppressive temperatures.

title: Caen's former prison goes on sale: 'It's strange, but it really has character'
subhed: Residents living near the former prison in Normandy, which has stood empty since 2023 and is now being put up for sale by the French state, have rallied to ensure it is not completely demolished. Buyers, who have until the end of May to submit their bids, will be required to prioritize the preservation of the historic building.

Why dropping the worst-case climate scenario does not undermine the reality of global warming
subhed: A new generation of climate scenarios has set aside RCP8.5, which projected a nearly 5°C rise in global temperatures by 2100. Donald Trump presents this as proof of 'alarmism' among climate scientists, but the new projections remain highly concerning.

title: Kyiv attacks: The complex logistics network behind Russia's war machine
subhed: The Ukrainian capital endured a barrage of strikes over the weekend, including ballistic missiles, cruise missiles and long-range drones. These increasingly frequent hybrid operations reveal the intensification of Russian drone and missile production.

title: The alleged murder plot casting a new shadow over French-Algerian ties
subhed: Four suspects in the attempted murder of Algerian opposition figure Hichem Aboud in Roubaix, northern France, in May 2025 were indicted. This threatens to complicate the resumed dialogue between Paris and Algiers.
